frontier_justification: Single-cell foundation models have become one of the hottest research frontiers in computational biology (2023-2025). The field has rapidly evolved from task-specific deep learning (scVI) to billion-parameter pretrained transformers (CellFM), driven by growing single-cell atlases and the success of language model pretraining. These models are reshaping how we analyze gene expression, predict perturbation effects, and discover therapeutic targets.

papers.title: Deep generative modeling for single-cell transcriptomics
papers.key_contribution: scVI: first deep generative model for single-cell transcriptomics; uses a variational autoencoder to learn low-dimensional cell representations while accounting for batch effects and dropout, enabling scalable probabilistic analysis of scRNA-seq data.

idea_genome.problem_genome: Single-cell RNA-seq data suffer from technical noise (batch effects, dropout events, variable library size), and traditional dimensionality reduction methods (PCA, t-SNE) lack a principled probabilistic framework to handle these confounders at scale.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Hierarchical Bayesian model with conditional distributions parameterized by deep neural networks (variational autoencoder); encodes each cell's transcriptome into a low-dimensional latent space while modeling library size and batch as explicit latent variables; trained via stochastic variational inference.
idea_genome.observation_genome: scVI achieves high accuracy on batch correction, clustering, visualization, and differential expression; scales to millions of cells; becomes the foundation of the scvi-tools ecosystem widely adopted in the single-cell community.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: VAE architecture limits expressiveness for capturing complex gene-gene dependencies; no large-scale pretraining on diverse cell atlases; requires task-specific model design; cannot transfer learned representations to new datasets or tasks without retraining.

papers.title: scBERT as a large-scale pretrained deep language model for cell type annotation of single-cell RNA-seq data
papers.key_contribution: scBERT: first Transformer-based pretrained model for single-cell RNA-seq; adapts BERT masked language modeling to gene expression tokens, pretrained on ~1 million cells and fine-tuned for cell type annotation with superior performance and robustness to batch effects.

idea_genome.problem_genome: Cell type annotation of scRNA-seq data requires large labeled datasets; existing methods (scVI, scANVI) are generative but not pretrained at scale; NLP breakthroughs in BERT-style pretraining have not yet been adapted to single-cell biology.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Treats each gene as a token and applies BERT-style masked language modeling on gene expression profiles; pretrained on ~1M unlabeled single-cell transcriptomes from public databases; fine-tuned with limited labeled data for cell type annotation.
idea_genome.observation_genome: scBERT outperforms existing methods on cell type annotation benchmarks; demonstrates robustness to batch effects; the pretraining-finetuning paradigm transfers well to novel cell types and datasets.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Pretrained on only ~1M cells (small by later standards); limited to cell type annotation as the primary task; BERT-style bidirectional attention is compute-intensive; no multi-omic data integration capability.

papers.title: Transfer learning enables predictions in network biology
papers.key_contribution: Geneformer: context-aware transformer pretrained on ~30 million single-cell transcriptomes; learns network hierarchy via self-supervised rank-value encoding of gene expression; transfer learning enables predictions in network biology including therapeutic target discovery for cardiomyopathy.

idea_genome.problem_genome: Predicting gene network dynamics and therapeutic targets requires large labeled datasets that are scarce in network biology; the pretrain-then-finetune paradigm from NLP could address this but requires scaling single-cell pretraining 30× beyond scBERT.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Rank-value encoding: genes are ranked by expression level within each cell and tokenized; transformer pretrained via masked gene prediction on 30M single-cell transcriptomes from Genecorpus-30M; attention weights encode gene network hierarchy in a fully self-supervised manner.
idea_genome.observation_genome: Geneformer accurately predicts gene dosage sensitivity, chromatin dynamics, and network interactions; fine-tuned on limited patient data, it identifies candidate therapeutic targets for cardiomyopathy validated by experiments.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Encoder-only model with no generative capability; limited to transcriptomics (no multi-omic integration); requires task-specific fine-tuning for each downstream application; rank-value encoding discards absolute expression magnitude information.

papers.title: scGPT: toward building a foundation model for single-cell multi-omics using generative AI
papers.key_contribution: scGPT: first GPT-style generative pretrained transformer for single-cell biology; pretrained autoregressively on 33 million cells; supports multi-task fine-tuning for cell annotation, perturbation prediction, multi-omic integration, and gene network inference.

idea_genome.problem_genome: Geneformer and scBERT use bidirectional (BERT-style) pretraining which cannot generate new cell states; the autoregressive GPT paradigm could enable generative capabilities (cell state generation, perturbation response prediction) but has not been applied to single-cell data at scale.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Autoregressive transformer pretrained on 33M cells from CellXGene; gene tokens are generated left-to-right conditioned on preceding genes; supports multi-task fine-tuning with task-specific prompts; integrates multi-omic data (RNA-seq + ATAC-seq) via shared embedding space.
idea_genome.observation_genome: scGPT achieves competitive or superior performance on cell annotation, multi-batch integration, perturbation prediction, and gene network inference; the autoregressive paradigm enables zero-shot cell generation and conditional generation.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Autoregressive generation is slower than bidirectional encoding for inference; unidirectional attention misses some bidirectional gene-gene dependencies; perturbation prediction accuracy remains moderate; the 33M cell pretraining corpus may be insufficient for rare cell types.

papers.title: Large-scale foundation model on single-cell transcriptomics
papers.key_contribution: scFoundation (xTrimoGene): 100-million-parameter asymmetric transformer pretrained on over 50 million human single-cell transcriptomic profiles; introduces a read-depth recovery pretraining task beyond standard masked language modeling to handle technical variability across datasets.

idea_genome.problem_genome: Existing single-cell FMs (scBERT, Geneformer, scGPT) use standard masked prediction but ignore the critical read-depth variability across sequencing protocols and datasets; a more biologically grounded pretraining objective is needed alongside architectural scale-up.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Asymmetric transformer architecture (xTrimoGene) with separate encoder and decoder; dual pretraining: masked gene expression prediction + read-depth recovery task that harmonizes read-depth differences across datasets; covers ~20,000 genes; pretrained on 50M cells.
idea_genome.observation_genome: scFoundation achieves state-of-the-art on gene expression enhancement, tissue drug response prediction, cell type annotation, and perturbation prediction; the read-depth recovery task significantly improves cross-dataset generalization.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Encoder-focused architecture; 100M parameters require substantial compute; limited to transcriptomic data (no epigenomic or proteomic integration); diminishing returns from dataset scale-up suggest architectural innovations may matter more than data scale.

papers.title: CellFM: a large-scale foundation model pre-trained on transcriptomics of 100 million human cells
papers.key_contribution: CellFM: 800-million-parameter foundation model pretrained on 100 million human cells using a modified RetNet (ERetNet) architecture with linear complexity; represents an 8× parameter and 2× data scale-up over scFoundation; outperforms all prior single-cell FMs on cell annotation, perturbation prediction, and gene function prediction.

idea_genome.problem_genome: Prior single-cell FMs (scFoundation: 100M params/50M cells) showed strong performance but the Transformer's quadratic attention complexity limits further scaling; a more efficient architecture is needed to scale to hundreds of millions of parameters and cells.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: ERetNet (Enhanced Retention Network) backbone with linear complexity replaces standard Transformer attention; pretrained on 100M human cells (2× largest prior dataset) with 800M parameters (8× scFoundation); trained on MindSpore platform for efficiency.
idea_genome.observation_genome: CellFM outperforms scGPT, Geneformer, and scFoundation across cell annotation, perturbation prediction, gene function prediction, and gene-gene relationship capturing; demonstrates that efficient architectures enable further beneficial scaling.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Enormous computational cost for training (800M params on 100M cells); RetNet architecture is less established than Transformer in the biology community; still limited to transcriptomics; question of whether further scaling yields proportional gains remains open.

edges.evolution_focus: scBERT diverges from scVI's variational autoencoder paradigm to a fundamentally different model family — Transformer with BERT-style masked language modeling — for single-cell analysis, splitting the field into generative (VAE) and discriminative pretrained (Transformer) lineages.

Geneformer applies the same BERT masked-prediction paradigm as scBERT but scales pretraining data 30× (1M → 30M cells) and introduces rank-value gene encoding, extending downstream applications from cell annotation to network biology and therapeutic target discovery.

scGPT diverges from Geneformer's bidirectional BERT-style pretraining to autoregressive GPT-style generation — a fundamentally different pretraining paradigm that enables generative capabilities (cell state generation, conditional perturbation prediction) not possible with encoder-only models.

scFoundation hybridizes BERT-style masked pretraining (from scBERT/Geneformer lineage) with a novel read-depth recovery task unique to single-cell biology, and uses an asymmetric encoder-decoder architecture (xTrimoGene) that combines elements from both bidirectional and generative paradigms.

CellFM performs an 8× parameter scale-up (100M → 800M) and 2× data scale-up (50M → 100M cells), replacing the Transformer backbone with RetNet (linear complexity) for efficiency — an architectural refinement within the same large-scale pretraining paradigm established by scFoundation.

open_question: CellFM achieves state-of-the-art performance through massive scale (800M parameters, 100M cells) but remains limited to transcriptomics. What mechanism would enable a truly multi-modal single-cell foundation model that jointly pretrains on transcriptomics, epigenomics (ATAC-seq, methylation), proteomics (CITE-seq), and spatial transcriptomics within a unified framework, while preserving cell-type-specific resolution and enabling cross-modal generative transfer?

question.name: Question
question.description: Present only the domain/subfield and the curated May-2026 research question; ask the model to propose a next-step research direction in Computational Biology / Single-Cell Genomics.

library.name: Library
library.description: Present the curated research frontier plus 6 trace papers in random order with paper titles and brief abstracts, but without explicit lineage order, genome fields, or dynamics labels; ask the model to propose the next research direction.

lineage.name: Lineage
lineage.description: Present the curated research frontier plus the ordered trace with genome metadata, GenomeDiff-style change evidence, and parent-child relationships; ask the model to predict the next evolutionary step without naming predefined category labels.

frontier_evidence.title: CellFM: a large-scale foundation model pre-trained on transcriptomics of 100 million human cells
frontier_evidence.unresolved_gap: Enormous computational cost for training (800M params on 100M cells); RetNet architecture is less established than Transformer in the biology community; still limited to transcriptomics; question of whether further scaling yields proportional gains remains open.
frontier_evidence.why_frontier: Used as the May 2026 frontier anchor: the arena question targets a limitation that remains unresolved at the endpoint of this lineage.

frontier_status.manual_review: Re-audited on 2026-05-05 for appendix release: active arena trace has a 2025/2026 frontier endpoint, a non-solved open research question, valid forward lineage edges, and generation settings aligned with post-hoc dynamics evaluation.
frontier_status.prompt_role: The same research question is shown in Question, Library, and Lineage settings; only the supporting literature/lineage context changes.
